outcome_signals.description: A real task assignment reaches a user-visible terminal outcome.
Provider repair or recovery evidence is visible when a provider cannot proceed.
A permission-gated Claude command produces a structured approval request, receives a web decision, resumes, and reaches a user-visible execution outcome.
The first real-time event is observable after assignment.
A user-facing failure path is captured as product evidence instead of raw provider text.
Operational monitoring can separate daemon, desktop, and frontend traffic anomalies with OTel/X-Ray evidence.
Real-screen and desktop checks catch copy, agent-name, and task-body-only behavior drift before beta handoff.
Daemon waits, backs off, and recovers during internet loss, full outage, and control-plane recovery surge.
Stress evidence records concurrent users, one-PC agent capacity, and cold-start packet burst limits.
Chaos evidence records restart recovery, scale-out behavior, and scale-out readiness duration.
Release evidence proves backend and daemon readiness, fresh install, latest assets, and temporary scenario gates.

loop.observation: Local QA, loop registry, and provider evidence exist, but product-success closure still required a human to connect meta complexity, product outcomes, and partial candidate debt.
loop.hypothesis: A product-loop evidence sidecar can turn those three concerns into measurable evidence and closed-loop candidates without changing runtime contracts.
loop.execute: Scan tool/workflow/verifier/generated-doc counts, entrypoint route coverage, claim mapping coverage, local acceptance scenario bindings, inferred execution ids, candidate loops, promotion count, and local QA run presence.
loop.evaluate: The report is passed only when meta complexity is routed, every outcome signal is bound to scenarios, and partial candidate debt has promotion and freshness evidence.
loop.retrospective: Harnesses keep finding failures while this sidecar shows which evidence must be promoted into stricter closed loops before humans can leave the loop.
